hard_negative_inventory.class: thenable assimilation and custom Promise-like receivers
executor callback timing and thrown executor errors

policy.note: Counts price boundary surfaces; they do not prove exact semantics.

recommended_order.next_action: reporting-only split and hard-negative expansion before exact admission
recommended_order.why: Promise aggregate semantics are already corpus-priced and have adjacent first/all-settled hard negatives.

recommended_order.next_action: reporting-only split and hard-negative expansion before exact admission
recommended_order.why: new Promise is high-risk because timing, resolve/reject callback, and throw-to-rejection behavior interact.

recommended_order.why: Cancellation appears across JS/TS scheduling APIs and must stay separate from fulfillment/rejection recovery.

surfaces.note: await is scheduling and thenable assimilation, not sync value equivalence
Swift throws/try is an explicit error channel
defer has scope-exit ordering and panic interaction semantics
async functions have Promise scheduling and rejection boundaries even without explicit await
Swift await has task scheduling and actor/lifetime boundaries
Rust .await polls a Future and must keep wake/scheduling effects explicit
channel send/receive has blocking and synchronization semantics
async fn/block creates a Future boundary
raise/rescue changes error channels and non-local control
Swift async surfaces create task/future-like protocol boundaries
Executor/Future APIs introduce scheduler and lifecycle boundaries
yield has suspension and iterator lifecycle semantics
Java streams need lazy/eager lifecycle and terminal materialization proof
go statements spawn concurrent execution
select has readiness, default, and scheduling semantics
Python await has coroutine scheduling and exception channel semantics
async def creates coroutine protocol boundaries
Ruby yield invokes a block with demand/effect obligations
new Promise needs executor timing, resolve/reject callback, and throw-to-rejection contracts
async for/with needs awaitable lifecycle and cleanup proof
Promise.all needs ordered all-fulfilled value and first rejection semantics
spawn APIs introduce scheduler, cancellation, and join-handle boundaries
CompletableFuture needs success/error channel and scheduling proof
AbortSignal/AbortController can change scheduling and rejection outcomes
Task APIs introduce scheduler and cancellation boundaries
asyncio task/sleep APIs create scheduler and cancellation boundaries
join/select style macros need all/first completion and cancellation proof
setInterval and timers interval streams have repeated emission, cancellation, and liveness semantics
Thread/Fiber APIs create scheduler and lifecycle boundaries
pthread_create introduces thread scheduling and lifetime boundaries
Promise.race needs first-settled ordering and liveness proof
asyncio gather/wait need aggregate completion, cancellation, and exception semantics
Promise.allSettled needs settled-record channel and shape proof
setjmp/longjmp is non-local control flow, not ordinary return
scheduler.yield needs microtask/order proof
Promise.any needs first-fulfilled and AggregateError rejection proof
